expect: c1_rate at S470 will be <=3.5% (dilution from lane count growth). Collaborate continuous will exceed 1.5, breaking margin warning. L-824x L-950 bridge: compliance-quality gap explains why discrete score stays 2 despite high merge_rate.
actual: c1_rate with strict methodology: 0.6% (6/933 lanes). S429 rate was 3.8% — methodologically inflated by conflating evolutionary supersession with concurrent duplication. Corrected Collaborate: 1.40 -> 2.70 (+1.30). Overall composite: 84% -> 95% (+11pp). Post-S430 strict C1: 0/112 = 0.0% — claim.py eliminated concurrent duplication entirely. L-824xL-950 bridge CONFIRMED: stale baseline = measurement compliance substituting for quality (L-824); eval system self-applied without re-examining instrument (L-950 fixed-point).
diff: Predicted <=3.5% dilution: WRONG mechanism — rate dropped from 3.8% to 0.6% via methodological correction, not dilution. Predicted continuous > 1.5: CONFIRMED at 2.70 (exceeded by 1.2). Predicted L-824xL-950 bridge: CONFIRMED — measurement inertia is the compliance-quality gap applied to evaluation instruments.

recombination.L-824_x_L-950: Compliance outruns quality (L-824) + self-application fixed-point (L-950) = measurement inertia. Stale baselines persist because the eval system applies itself without questioning instrument validity. The score becomes the reality.
recombination.L-1165_connection: c1 baseline was a false instrument (L-1165: 33% rate). It measured evolutionary churn, not concurrent duplication. Same pattern as F-RAND1 Gini criterion and F-NK6 documentation-only intervention.

corrective_action: Created f-con1-baseline-s470.json with strict C1 methodology. eval_sufficiency_data.py auto-loads latest baseline (glob picks s470 over s429).
self_apply: This measurement corrected its own scoring system — demonstrating that eval IS structurally responsive to evidence (the lane's self-apply prediction).
